item.text: I’ll inspect the TLA+ module and existing lemmas, then iteratively patch only the placeholder proof and run TLAPS/checks until clean.

item.aggregated_output:
-------------------------- MODULE Allocator_NextMutex -----------------------------

(***********************************************************************)
(* Specification of an allocator managing a set of resources:          *)
(* - Clients can request sets of resources whenever all their previous *)
(*   requests have been satisfied.                                     *)
(* - Requests can be partly fulfilled, and resources can be returned   *)
(*   even before the full request has been satisfied. However, clients *)
(*   only have an obligation to return resources after they have       *)
(*   obtained all resources they requested.                            *)
(*                                                                     *)
(* The proofs in this module were written before TLAPS's SMT backend   *)
(* prover was implemented. Much shorter proofs can be obtained using   *)
(* that backend.                                                       *)
(***********************************************************************)

\* EXTENDS FiniteSets, TLC

CONSTANTS
  Client,     \* set of all clients
  Resource    \* set of all resources

\* ASSUME
\*  IsFiniteSet(Resource)

VARIABLES
  unsat,       \* set of all outstanding requests per process
  alloc        \* set of resources allocated to given process

TypeInvariant ==
  /\ unsat \in [Client -> SUBSET Resource]
  /\ alloc \in [Client -> SUBSET Resource]

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

(* Resource are available iff they have not been allocated. *)
available == Resource \ (UNION {alloc[c] : c \in Client})

(* Initially, no resources have been requested or allocated. *)
Init ==
  /\ unsat = [c \in Client |-> {}]
  /\ alloc = [c \in Client |-> {}]

(**********************************************************************)
(* A client c may request a set of resources provided that all of its *)
(* previous requests have been satisfied and that it doesn't hold any *)
(* resources.                                                         *)
(**********************************************************************)
Request(c,S) ==
  /\ unsat[c] = {} /\ alloc[c] = {}
  /\ S # {} /\ unsat' = [unsat EXCEPT ![c] = S]
  /\ UNCHANGED alloc

(*******************************************************************)
(* Allocation of a set of available resources to a client that     *)
(* requested them (the entire request does not have to be filled). *)
(*******************************************************************)
Allocate(c,S) ==
  /\ S # {} /\ S \subseteq available \cap unsat[c]
  /\ alloc' = [alloc EXCEPT ![c] = alloc[c] \cup S]
  /\ unsat' = [unsat EXCEPT ![c] = unsat[c] \ S]

(*******************************************************************)
(* Client c returns a set of resources that it holds. It may do so *)
(* even before its full request has been honored.                  *)
(*******************************************************************)
Return(c,S) ==
  /\ S # {} /\ S \subseteq alloc[c]
  /\ alloc' = [alloc EXCEPT ![c] = alloc[c] \ S]
  /\ UNCHANGED unsat

(* The next-state relation. *)
Next ==
  \E c \in Client, S \in SUBSET Resource :
     Request(c,S) \/ Allocate(c,S) \/ Return(c,S)

vars == <<unsat,alloc>>

-------------------------------------------------------------------------


(* The complete high-level specification. *)
SimpleAllocator ==
  /\ Init /\ [][Next]_vars
  /\ \A c \in Client: WF_vars(Return(c, alloc[c]))
  /\ \A c \in Client: SF_vars(\E S \in SUBSET Resource: Allocate(c,S))

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

Mutex ==
  \A c1,c2 \in Client : \A r \in Resource :
     r \in alloc[c1] \cap alloc[c2] => c1 = c2

ClientsWillReturn ==
  \A c \in Client : unsat[c]={} ~> alloc[c]={}

ClientsWillObtain ==
  \A c \in Client, r \in Resource : r \in unsat[c] ~> r \in alloc[c]

InfOftenSatisfied ==
  \A c \in Client : []<>(unsat[c] = {})

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

(* Used for symmetry reduction with TLC *)
\* Symmetry == Permutations(Client) \cup Permutations(Resource)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

(**********************************************************************)
(* The following version states a weaker fairness requirement for the *)
(* clients: resources need be returned only if the entire request has *)
(* been satisfied.                                                    *)
(**********************************************************************)

(*

SimpleAllocator2 ==
  /\ Init /\ [][Next]_vars
  /\ \A c \in Client: WF_vars(unsat[c] = {} /\ Return(c, alloc[c]))
  /\ \A c \in Client: SF_vars(\E S \in SUBSET Resource: Allocate(c,S))

*)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM InitTypeInvariant == Init => TypeInvariant
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM RequestTypeInvariant ==
  ASSUME NEW c \in Client,
         NEW S \in SUBSET Resource
  PROVE  TypeInvariant /\ Request(c,S) => TypeInvariant'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM AllocateTypeInvariant ==
  ASSUME NEW c \in Client,
         NEW S \in SUBSET Resource
  PROVE  TypeInvariant /\ Allocate(c,S) => TypeInvariant'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM ReturnTypeInvariant ==
  ASSUME NEW c \in Client,
         NEW S \in SUBSET Resource
  PROVE  TypeInvariant /\ Return(c,S) => TypeInvariant'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM NextTypeInvariant == TypeInvariant /\ Next => TypeInvariant'
  PROOF OMITTED

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

THEOREM InitMutex == Init => Mutex
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM RequestMutexBis ==
  ASSUME NEW clt \in Client,
         NEW S \in SUBSET Resource
  PROVE  Mutex /\ Request(clt,S) => Mutex'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM ReturnMutex ==
  ASSUME NEW clt \in Client,
         NEW S \in SUBSET Resource
  PROVE  TypeInvariant /\ Mutex /\ Return(clt,S) => Mutex'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM AllocateMutex ==
  ASSUME NEW clt \in Client,
         NEW S \in SUBSET Resource
  PROVE  TypeInvariant /\ Mutex /\ Allocate(clt, S) => Mutex'
  PROOF OMITTED

THEOREM NextMutex == TypeInvariant /\ Mutex /\ Next => Mutex'
PROOF OBVIOUS

item.text: I found the final theorem reduces directly by case analysis on `Next`, using the three admitted action-preservation lemmas.

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: Allocator_NextMutex.tla
@@
 THEOREM NextMutex == TypeInvariant /\\ Mutex /\\ Next => Mutex'
-PROOF OBVIOUS
+PROOF
+  ASSUME TypeInvariant /\\ Mutex /\\ Next
+  PROVE  Mutex'
+  <1>1. Next
+    OBVIOUS
+  <1>2. TypeInvariant
+    OBVIOUS
+  <1>3. Mutex
+    OBVIOUS
+  <1>4. PICK clt \\in Client, S \\in SUBSET Resource :
+          Request(clt,S) \\/ Allocate(clt,S) \\/ Return(clt,S)
+    BY <1>1 DEF Next
+  <1>5. CASE Request(clt,S)
+    <2>1. Mutex'
+      BY <1>3, <1>5, RequestMutexBis
+    <2> QED
+      BY <2>1
+  <1>6. CASE Allocate(clt,S)
+    <2>1. Mutex'
+      BY <1>2, <1>3, <1>6, AllocateMutex
+    <2> QED
+      BY <2>1
+  <1>7. CASE Return(clt,S)
+    <2>1. Mutex'
+      BY <1>2, <1>3, <1>7, ReturnMutex
+    <2> QED
+      BY <2>1
+  <1> QED
+    BY <1>4, <1>5, <1>6, <1>7
 
 =========================================================================
*** End Patch
PATCH
~/.tlapm15/bin/tlapm -I ~/.tlapm15/lib/tlaps Allocator_NextMutex.tla"

(*                                                                         *)
(* This is a standard invariance proof, where <1>2 asserts that any step   *)
(* of the algorithm (including a stuttering step) starting in a state in   *)
(* which Inv is true leaves Inv true.  Step <1>4 follows easily from       *)
(* <1>1-<1>3 by simple temporal reasoning.                                 *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM Spec => []Inv
<1> USE DEFS TypeOK, Inv, IInv, ProcSet
<1>1. Init => Inv
  BY DEFS Init, Inv
<1>2. Inv /\ [Next]_vars => Inv'
  <2> SUFFICES ASSUME Inv, [Next]_vars PROVE Inv'
    OBVIOUS
  <2> USE DEFS After, LL
  <2>1. ASSUME NEW self \in P, p1(self) PROVE Inv'
    BY <2>1 DEF p1
  <2>2. ASSUME NEW self \in P, p2(self) PROVE Inv'
    <3>1. CASE unread[self] = {}
      BY <2>2, <3>1 DEF p2
    <3>2. CASE unread[self] # {}
      BY <2>2, <3>2 DEF p2
(* TypeOK is an inductive invariant *)
LEMMA TypeCorrect == Spec => []TypeOK
<1>. USE DEF TypeOK, Nodes, Color
<1>1. Init => TypeOK
  BY DEF Init
<1>2. TypeOK /\ [Next]_vars => TypeOK'
(* FIXME: Although each of the steps below is proved instantly, 
   the attempt to prove the assertion all at once fails??
  BY DEF Next, vars, Controlled, Environment, InitiateProbe,
         PassToken, SendMsg, Deactivate
*)
  <2> SUFFICES ASSUME TypeOK,
                      [Next]_vars
               PROVE  TypeOK'
    OBVIOUS
  <2>1. CASE InitiateProbe
(* satisfies the formula Next, and TypeOK /\ Inv is true in the first      *)
(* state, then TypeOK /\ Inv is true in the second state.                  *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM Invariance == TypeOK /\ Inv /\ Next => TypeOK' /\ Inv'
<1>1. CASE UNCHANGED vars
  BY <1>1 DEF TypeOK, Inv, Next, ProcSet, p, vars
<1>2. ASSUME TypeOK, Inv,
             NEW i \in {0,1},
             a(i) \/ b(i) \/ cs(i)
      PROVE  TypeOK'
    BY <1>2, IsaM("auto") DEF TypeOK, Inv, a, b, cs
<1>3. ASSUME TypeOK, TypeOK', Inv,
             NEW i \in {0,1},
             a(i) \/ b(i) \/ cs(i),
             NEW j \in {0,1},
             Inv!(i) /\ Inv!(j)

<2>1. CASE InitiateProbe
    BY <2>1 DEF InitiateProbe 
  <2>2. ASSUME NEW i \in Nodes \ {0},
               PassToken(i)
        PROVE  TypeOK'
    BY <2>2 DEF PassToken 
  <2>3. ASSUME NEW i \in Nodes,
               Deactivate(i)
        PROVE  TypeOK'
    BY <2>3 DEF Deactivate
  <2>4. ASSUME NEW i \in Nodes,
               SendMsg(i)
        PROVE  TypeOK'
    BY <2>4 DEF SendMsg
  <2>5. CASE UNCHANGED vars
    BY <2>5 DEF vars
  <2>6. QED
    BY <2>1, <2>2, <2>3, <2>4, <2>5 DEF Controlled, Environment, Next
<1>3. QED
  BY <1>1, <1>2, PTL DEF Spec



THEOREM Spec => []TerminationDetection
(* Dijkstra's invariant implies correctness *)
<1>1 Inv => TerminationDetection
  BY  DEF Inv, TerminationDetection, terminationDetected, Nodes
(* Dijkstra's invariant is (trivially) established by the initial condition *)
<1>2 Init => Inv
  BY DEF Init, Inv
(* Dijkstra's invariant is inductive relative to the type invariant *)
<1>3 TypeOK /\ Inv /\ [Next]_vars => Inv'
  BY DEF Inv, TypeOK, Next, vars, Controlled, Environment,
         InitiateProbe, PassToken, SendMsg, Deactivate, Nodes, Color
<1> QED  BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>3, TypeCorrect, PTL DEF Spec


(* If the one-line proof of step <1>1 above is too obscure, 
    here is a more detailed, hierarchical proof of the same property. *)
LEMMA Inv_implies_Termination == Inv => TerminationDetection
<1>1. ASSUME tpos = 0, 
             Inv!(i) /\ Inv!(j)
      PROVE  Inv!(j)'
  <2>1. /\ 1-i \in {0, 1}
        /\ 1-j \in {0, 1}
        /\ i # j => /\ 1-i = j
                    /\ 1-j = i
    BY IsaM("auto")
  <2>2. USE DEF TypeOK, Inv
  <2>3. CASE a(i)
     BY <2>1, <1>3, <2>3 DEF a
  <2>4. CASE b(i)
    <3>1. CASE i = j
      BY <2>1, <1>3, <2>4, <3>1 DEF b
    <3>2. CASE i # j
       BY <2>1, <1>3, <2>4, <3>2 DEF b
    <3>3. QED
      BY <3>1, <3>2
  <2>5. CASE cs(i)
      BY <2>1, <2>5, <1>3 DEF cs
  <2>6. QED
     BY <2>3, <2>4, <2>5, <1>3
<1>4. QED
  BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>3 DEF Next, p, ProcSet, Inv

(****************************************************************************)
(* The same theorem proved with the help of the SMT backend.                *)
(* The skeleton of the proof was generated with the help of the command     *)
(* "Decompose Proof" (C-G C-D) available in the TLA+ Toolbox.               *)
(****************************************************************************)
THEOREM TypeOK /\ Inv /\ Next => TypeOK' /\ Inv'
\*  BY DEF TypeOK, Inv, Next, ProcSet, p, vars, a, b, cs
  <1> SUFFICES ASSUME TypeOK,
                      Inv,
                      Next
               PROVE  TypeOK' /\ Inv'
    OBVIOUS
  <1>. USE DEF Inv, TypeOK
  <1>1. ASSUME NEW self \in {0,1},
               a(self)
        PROVE  TypeOK' /\ Inv'
    BY <1>1 DEF a
  <1>2. ASSUME NEW self \in {0,1},
               b(self)
        PROVE  TypeOK' /\ Inv'
    BY <1>2 DEF b
  <1>3. ASSUME NEW self \in {0,1},

<1>3. ASSUME NEW self \in {0,1},
               cs(self)
        PROVE  TypeOK' /\ Inv'
    BY <1>3 DEF cs
  <1>4. CASE UNCHANGED vars
    BY <1>4 DEF vars
  <1>5. QED
    BY <1>1, <1>2, <1>3, <1>4 DEF Next, p

----------------------------------------------------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(* The following is a trivial consequence of the Invariance theorem, the   *)
(* definition of [Next]_vars, and the fact that UNCHANGED vars implies     *)
(* that none of the declared variables changes.                            *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM TLAInvariance == TypeOK /\ Inv /\ [Next]_vars => TypeOK' /\ Inv'
BY Invariance DEF TypeOK, Inv, Next, ProcSet, p, vars

(***************************************************************************)
(* The following theorem asserts that the mutual exclusion property is     *)
(* always verified by the system.                                          *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM Safety == Spec => []MutualExclusion
  BY Initialization, TLAInvariance, Mutex, PTL DEF Spec
